calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2020 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Mentorboxs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-20-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT MENTORBOX LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is Off calendar pursuant to notice of dismissal of entire action filed 2/3/20.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael G. Jaco, Esq., Kesluk, Silverstein & Jacob, P.C., 310-273-3180, appearing for Plaintiff Jeremy; (CourtCall)
E. Jeffrey Banchero, Esq., Banchero Law Firm LLP, 415-955-9101, appearing for Defendant Mentor Box, LLC;

All Parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows as modified:

DEFENDANT MENTORBOX LLC'S Motion To Compel Answers To First Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions.

Motion GRANTED. Answers to interrogatories shall be "as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits." CCP 2030.220(a). "If only part of an interrogatory is objectionable, the remainder of the interrogatory shall be answered." CCP 2030.240(a). And, if a party objects to "an interrogatory or part of an interrogatory, the specific ground for the objection shall be set forth clearly in the response." CCP 2030.240(b). Boilerplate objections are inappropriate and "may be sanctionable." Korea Data Systems Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 51 Cal.App.4th 1513, 1516. The original answers were boilerplate objections. Because substantive answers were served after the agreed date of 9/13 but before the hearing, I will consider the answers. SI 8, 9: Granted. Plaintiff must describe the questions or state that he cannot comply. CCP 2030.220(a). SI 11, 12, 13, 14: Granted. Interrogatories may call for legal conclusions. CCP 2030.010(b); Rifkind v. Super. Ct. (1994) 22 Cal. App. 4th 1255, 1261. SI 15: Granted. Plaintiff must identify names of persons he remembers. On sanctions: The correspondence does not support Plaintiff's assertion that the parties agreed to stay of discovery to discuss settlement. Banchero Decl. Ex. G disclaims an extension of discovery. Morrison Decl. Ex. B requests Defendant withdraw the motions if it receives "responses without objections." The supplemental answers repeated the boilerplate objections, plus qualified to say that additional responses are "subject to and without waiving the foregoing objections, and to the extent that Plaintiff understands this request," which is neither Code-compliant nor "without objections." Thus, I do not find Plaintiff acted with substantial justification. However, because the motions are "patterned after" each other, the amount of hours appears excessive. Sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $7,635 (10 hours for Mr. Banchero at $645/hour + 3 hours for Ms. Banchero at $395/hour), payable by 11/29. CCP 2030.300, CRC 3.1348(a).
Payable by firm, not individual attorneys. Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-04-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael G. Jaco, Esq., Kesluk, Silverstein & Jacob, P.C., 310-273-3180, appearing for Plaintiff Jeremy; (CourtCall)
E. Jeffrey Banchero, Esq., Banchero Law Firm LLP, 415-955-9101, appearing for Defendant Mentor Box, LLC;

All Parties stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows as modified:

DEFENDANT MENTORBOX LLC'S Motion To Compel Answers To Requests For Admissions And For Sanctions

Motion GRANTED in part. Responses to the RFAs were not served by the agreed date of 9/13. The email of 9/18 was a request to extend the deadline but does not operate as a stipulation to extend a deadline that already had passed. However, after the motion was filed but before this hearing, Plaintiff served proposed responses that are in substantial compliance with CCP 2033.220 - except with respect to RFA 3. Plaintiff shall provide a revised response to RFA 3 that complies with CCP 2033.220. When responses are served late, CCP 2033.280(c) makes an award of sanctions "mandatory." As with the motion on interrogatories, given the overlap between motions, the amount of hours requested appears excessive. Sanctions are GRANTED in the amount of $7,635 (10 hours for Mr. Banchero at $645/hour + 3 hours for Ms. Banchero at $395/hour), payable by 11/29. CCP 2033.280, CRC 3.1348(a). Sanction against firm not individual attorneys. Order is signed. Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
